findings.evidence: New wrappers at lines 68, 76, 87, 92, 99, 104, 110; existing APIs are `IsSemigroupGroupPD.sum_nonneg`/`quadForm_two_nonneg`/`conj_symm`/`diagonal_*` in `SemigroupGroup.lean:238-275`, and generic kernel facts in `Kernel.lean:84-101` plus `KernelBounds.lean:60-61`.
findings.fix: Keep `timeSlice_isPositiveDefiniteKernel` and derive downstream uses from `isPositiveDefiniteKernel_iff`, `isPositiveDefiniteKernel_conj_symm`, `isPositiveDefiniteKernel_apply_self_nonneg`, `isPositiveDefiniteKernel_normSq_le`, or the existing BCR `sum_nonneg`/`diagonal_*` lemmas; remove the parallel wrappers unless a downstream theorem requires these exact names.
findings.issue: `timeSlice_sum_nonneg` through `timeSlice_normSq_le` mostly restate existing quadratic-form, symmetry, diagonal, and Cauchy-Schwarz APIs under new names.

summary: The core fixed-time kernel pullback is not duplicated, but most of the follow-on slice lemmas create a parallel API for existing kernel/BCR consequences. Trim those wrappers or justify why this namespace needs duplicate names instead of reusing the generic facts after `timeSlice_isPositiveDefiniteKernel`.
